articles.title: Iran briefing: Before the ceasefire, Trump resumed dropping f-bombs

children.content: Donald Trump is reported to unleash another expletive-laden outburst at Benjamin Netanyahu over Israeli strikes as the US and Iran finalise a peace deal.

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

articles.title: US and Iran announce ceasefire and plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The United States and Iran reach an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, offering relief to the global economy after three months of fighting.

image.altText: Trump speaks to reports in the oval office.

articles.title: ASX rallies, oil prices fall on US-Iran ceasefire agreement

children.content: The local share market and Australian dollar are rising after Donald Trump confirmed a ceasefire agreement had been reached with Iran, saying the Strait of Hormuz will open. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A trader looks up at a chart on his computer screen while working on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange.

children.content: Pakistan, which has been negotiating peace talks between the US and Iran, says a deal to end the war has been agreed. Follow live.

updates.title: Vance says deal will 'transform the Middle East'
UK, France, Germany and Italy prepared to lift sanctions
Anthony Albanese 'pleased' about US-Iran ceasefire agreement

items.title: PM welcomes Iran–US ceasefire, urges Tehran to address concerns on nuclear

children.content: Anthony Albanese welcomes a ceasefire reached between the US and Iran, but urges Tehran to address "longstanding concerns" about its nuclear program. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Outbreak of this Victorian-era disease labelled a 'health disaster and tragedy'

children.content: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

image.altText: A man inside a lab studying a human skull, a skeleton model hangs nearby.

items.title: Côte d'Ivoire joy as supersub scores last-minute winner against Ecuador

children.content: Côte d'Ivoire scores a last-minute winner against Ecuador after Japan finds a late leveller against the Netherlands and World Cup debutant Curaçao enjoys a magical moment in the midst of a drubbing. Follow live.

image.altText: Amad Diallo celebrates a goal for Cote d'Ivoire

items.title: 'Let them talk': Socceroos hero bites back at claim Türkiye 'dominated'

children.content: Socceroos goalscoring hero Nestory Irankunda says Türkiye could not back up its talk, despite one of its stars claiming post-game Australia had been "dominated".

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda looks at the ball in front of his face during a game

A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

children.content: US president Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 starts on the South Lawn of the White House. Follow live.

items.title: Note, unused life jackets left behind in murder-suicide at Sydney bay

children.content: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

image.altText: A boat on a dock, with police tape in front and two officers examining the scene.

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing'

children.content: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

image.altText: Military vehicles carrying DF-26 ballistic missiles during a military parade in China.

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

items.title: Is SpaceX worth $3 trillion? Of course not, but that's not the point

children.content: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

items.title: Ireland is a data centre 'cautionary tale'. This is the impact on locals

children.content: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

items.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans for West Bank

children.content: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

children.content: Most experts are tipping interest rates will remain on hold but the question is when will they come down.

items.title: Violence victim unable to break lease without paying

children.content: Annie feared for her safety from a new next-door neighbour in northern Tasmania but was told she couldn't break her lease without continuing to pay rent. She was then sexually assaulted.

children.content: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

items.title: Singapore-based AI company to become Tasmania's largest power user

children.content: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

items.title: Beach's dream is Ryan's nightmare, and Schwarzer can relate to both

children.content: Pat Beach became a surprise national hero with his performance against Türkiye, while incumbent goalkeeper Mat Ryan watched from the bench. Mark Schwarzer has experienced both sides of that coin.

items.title: When Irankunda ran to the corner to emulate Cahill, it was a pre-planned tribute

items.title: Chemicals in Antarctic huts pose 'potential explosive' risk

children.content: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Ernest Shackleton and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warned.

items.title: Concerns new BHP agreement 'locks in' basin water extraction

children.content: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

image.altText: An overhead view of a BHP wellfield in outback South Australia.

items.title: Like a 'tin of sardines': The library programs at bursting point on Melbourne's fringe

children.content: Local councils and Public Libraries Victoria say the way public libraries are funded across Victoria needs reform, with communities in Melbourne's growth corridors described as "chronically underfunded".
